title: Alectra basutica
taxonomy: Alectra basutica is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a type of parasitic plant that relies on other plants for nutrients. Alectra basutica is native to the region of southern Africa, specifically in countries such as Lesotho and South Africa. The classification of Alectra basutica is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Lamiales. As a member of the family Orobanchaceae, Alectra basutica is closely related to other parasitic plants in the same family.

intro: Alectra basutica, also known as the African snake plant, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Orobanchaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Alectra basutica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, immunomodulatory, and anti-cancer activities. In traditional medicine, Alectra basutica is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of herbal teas, capsules, and ointments.
